days.title: Мога: I can (ability)

vocab.example: Аз мога да чета на български.

vocab.exampleEn: I can swim in the sea.

grammar.title: The verb мога + да + present
grammar.explanation: Bulgarian has no infinitive. To say 'I can do something' you use мога followed by да and a present-tense verb that agrees with the same subject: мога да чета (I can read). The full present of мога is: аз мога, ти можеш, той/тя може, ние можем, вие можете, те могат. Note the г/ж change: мога and могат keep -г-, while all the middle forms (можеш, може, можем, можете) have -ж-. Both verbs share one subject, so both are conjugated: тя може да готви (she can cook).

examples.english: I can swim.
You can drive a car.
She can cook well.

reading.title: Какво можеш да правиш?
reading.text: — Можеш ли да плуваш? — Да, мога да плувам добре. А ти можеш ли да караш колело?
reading.translation: — Can you swim? — Yes, I can swim well. And can you ride a bicycle?

days.speakingPrompt: List three things you can do well using 'мога да...' (e.g. swim, cook, drive). Then ask a friend 'Можеш ли да...?' for each.

days.title: Не мога: I cannot

vocab.example: Не мога да дойда, защото съм болен.
vocab.exampleEn: I can't come because I am ill.

vocab.example: Уморена съм и не мога да работя.
vocab.exampleEn: I am tired and cannot work.

grammar.title: Negation: не мога да...
grammar.explanation: To make мога negative, place не directly before it: не мога, не можеш, не може, не можем, не можете, не могат. The structure stays не мога + да + present verb: не мога да спя (I can't sleep). You can give a reason with защото (because) and remember Bulgarian uses the present tense of 'to be' съм: уморен съм (I am tired), той е болен (he is ill). The adjective agrees in gender: болен (m), болна (f), уморен (m), уморена (f).

examples.russian: Не мога да виждам без очила.
examples.english: I cannot see without glasses.

examples.russian: Тя не може да дойде, защото е болна.
examples.english: She cannot come because she is ill.
We cannot hear the music.

reading.title: Защо не можеш?
reading.text: — Можеш ли да работиш днес? — Не, не мога, защото съм болен. — А утре? — Утре може би.
reading.translation: — Can you work today? — No, I can't, because I am ill. — And tomorrow? — Tomorrow maybe.

days.speakingPrompt: Decline three invitations using 'Не мога да... защото...' and give a reason each time (you are tired, ill, busy).

days.title: Искам: I want

vocab.exampleEn: What do you want to eat?
I want to travel to the sea.
I want a glass of water.

grammar.title: The verb искам + да + present (and direct objects)
grammar.explanation: искам is a regular -а verb: аз искам, ти искаш, той/тя иска, ние искаме, вие искате, те искат. For a desired action use искам да + present: искам да пътувам (I want to travel). For a desired thing, use искам + noun directly: искам вода (I want water). Because Bulgarian has no noun cases, the noun does not change form — only word order and articles matter: искам водата (I want the water), искам книгата (I want the book).

examples.english: I want to rest.
Do you want water?
They want to buy a car.

reading.title: В кафенето
reading.text: — Какво искаш? — Искам чаша вода и кафе. — Аз искам само вода.
reading.translation: — What do you want? — I want a glass of water and a coffee. — I want only water.

days.speakingPrompt: Order in a cafe: say three things you want using 'Искам...' (a thing) and 'Искам да...' (an action, e.g. to rest, to pay).

days.title: Трябва: I must / have to

vocab.exampleEn: I have to get up early.
I get up early every day.
I have to pay the bill.

grammar.title: Impersonal трябва + да + present
grammar.explanation: трябва means 'it is necessary' and is impersonal — it does NOT change for person. The person is shown by the verb after да: трябва да работя (I must work), трябва да работиш (you must work), трябва да работим (we must work). The form трябва stays the same for everyone. Negative: не трябва да... means 'must not / should not': не трябва да бързаш (you shouldn't hurry).

examples.english: I have to get up early.
You have to pay the bill.
We shouldn't wait here.

reading.title: Сутрин
reading.text: Трябва да ставам рано, защото трябва да работя. Не мога да почивам, защото бързам.
reading.translation: I have to get up early because I have to work. I cannot rest because I am in a hurry.

days.speakingPrompt: Describe your morning duties: name four things you must do using 'Трябва да...' (get up early, hurry, pay a bill, wait).

days.title: Може ли?: May I? (permission)

vocab.example: Тук не може да се пуши.

grammar.title: Може ли да...? for permission
grammar.explanation: While може is the 'he/she/it' form of мога, on its own може is also impersonal and means 'it is allowed / one may'. To ask permission, use Може ли да + present: Може ли да вляза? (May I come in?). To grant: Да, може / Разбира се, може. To refuse: Не, не може / Съжалявам, не може. For general rules use the reflexive се: Тук не може да се пуши (Smoking isn't allowed here). Compare: мога = personal ability, може ли = asking if something is permitted.

examples.english: May I sit here?
Of course, you may.
I'm sorry, it's not allowed here.

reading.title: В ресторанта
reading.text: — Може ли да седя до прозореца? — Разбира се, може. — А може ли да пуша? — Не, съжалявам, тук не може.
reading.translation: — May I sit by the window? — Of course, you may. — And may I smoke? — No, I'm sorry, it's not allowed here.

days.speakingPrompt: Role-play asking for permission in three situations using 'Може ли да...?' (sit here, come in, help). Give a polite 'yes' and a polite 'no' answer for each.

days.title: All together: can, want, must, may

vocab.english: I need (it is needed to me)

vocab.exampleEn: I have to leave right away.

grammar.title: Combining the modals and 'трябва ми'
grammar.explanation: You can now choose the right modal by meaning: мога да = ability ('I can'), искам да = desire ('I want'), трябва да = necessity ('I must'), може ли да = permission ('may I'). There is also трябва ми + noun, meaning 'I need (something)', where the person is shown by a short pronoun: трябва ми (I need), трябва ти (you need), трябва му (he needs), трябва ѝ (she needs). The thing needed is the subject, so plural needs use трябват: трябват ми пари (I need money).

examples.russian: Искам да остана, но трябва да тръгна.
examples.english: I want to stay, but I have to leave.

examples.russian: Може ли да помогна? Трябва ти помощ.
examples.english: May I help? You need help.
I need a break right away.

reading.title: Трудно решение
reading.text: Искам да остана, но трябва да тръгна веднага. Не мога да чакам, защото наистина бързам.
reading.translation: I want to stay, but I have to leave right away. I cannot wait because I am really in a hurry.

days.speakingPrompt: Tell a short story about a busy day using all four modals at least once: something you can do, want to do, must do, and ask permission for. End with one 'Трябва ми...' sentence.

days.title: Review: Can, must, want

grammar.title: Recap: the four modal patterns
grammar.explanation: This week you learned four ways to express modality, all followed by да + a present-tense verb (Bulgarian has no infinitive). 1) мога да = ability ('I can'); conjugates fully (мога, можеш, може, можем, можете, могат); negate with не мога. 2) искам да = desire ('I want'); regular verb, also takes a noun directly (искам вода). 3) трябва да = necessity ('I must'); impersonal, never changes for person — the second verb shows who. 4) може ли да = permission ('may I?'); answer with разбира се, може or съжалявам, не може. Remember трябва ми + noun = 'I need', and always use съм/си/е for 'to be' (уморен съм). No noun cases: relations come from prepositions and word order.

examples.russian: Мога да плувам, но не мога да карам кола.
examples.english: I can swim, but I can't drive a car.

examples.russian: Искам да почивам, защото трябва да работя утре.
examples.english: I want to rest because I have to work tomorrow.

examples.russian: Може ли да седя тук? — Разбира се, може.
examples.english: May I sit here? — Of course, you may.

reading.title: Планове за деня
reading.text: — Какво искаш да правиш днес? — Искам да почивам, но трябва да работя. — Може ли да помогна? — Разбира се! Не мога да свърша всичко сам.
reading.translation: — What do you want to do today? — I want to rest, but I have to work. — May I help? — Of course! I can't finish everything alone.

days.speakingPrompt: Have a two-minute conversation about your weekend: say one thing you can do, one you want to do, one you must do, and ask permission for one thing. Use мога, искам, трябва, and може ли at least once each.
